---
title: "Interactive capacity map"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: false
---

Explore where Missoula's plan-enabled housing capacity clusters. Each
**hex** is scored by how much untapped capacity it *and its neighbors*
hold; green hexes hold significantly more than a random arrangement of
the same parcels would produce (a Getis-Ord Gi\* test). The three green
tiers are **confidence levels, not capacity levels** — darker green means
stronger statistical evidence that the concentration is real (99% / 95% /
90%). Toggle on the **opportunity parcels** layer to see the underlying
vacant and underbuilt lots (blue); click any parcel for its current use,
future land-use designation, acreage, and plan-enabled units.

```{=html}
<iframe src="output/maps/hotspot_map.html"
        style="width:100%; height:78vh; border:1px solid #ddd; border-radius:6px;">
</iframe>
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
Sources: City of Missoula taxlot layer (2024). Capacity is plan-implied
(Growth Policy future land use), not a development forecast — see
[Methods & Sources](methods.qmd).
:::
